spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for local-network-access-restrictions-for-websockets
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Local Network Access for WebSockets. WebSocket opening handshake invokes fetch, so LNA checks inherit automatically. No RequestInit on WebSocket → no targetAddressSpace declaration; mixed-content ws:// can't be bypassed. Public→local/loopback prompts user; deny → connection fails with network error. Edge cases: per-connection DNS rebinding protection; no opt-in path for declared local intent.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

summary: Workable concept with remaining spec-surface and failure-mode follow-ups. Major reference finding resolved.
